questions.q: A queue is an example of a linear data structure. What is the basic operation that can be performed on a queue?
questions.answer: The basic operation that can be performed on a queue is enqueue and dequeue.

questions.q: Consider a queue Q = {3, 6, 7} with operations as follows: Enqueue(5), Enqueue(9). What will be the new queue?
A queue Q has the following operations: Enqueue(A), Dequeue(), Enqueue(B), Dequeue(). What will be the final queue if initially Q = {1, 2, 3}?
questions.answer: Initially Q = {1, 2, 3}. After Dequeue(), Q = {2, 3}. After Enqueue(B), Q = {2, 3, B}. After Dequeue(), Q = {3, B}.

questions.q: What is the advantage of using a queue data structure?
questions.answer: The advantage of using a queue data structure is that it follows the First-In-First-Out (FIFO) principle.

questions.q: Consider a queue Q = {10, 20, 30} with operations as follows: Enqueue(15), Enqueue(40). What will be the new queue?
What is the time complexity of the enqueue operation in a queue?
A queue Q has the following operations: Enqueue(X), Dequeue(), Enqueue(Y), Dequeue(). What will be the final queue if initially Q = {5, 6, 7}?
questions.answer: Initially Q = {5, 6, 7}. After Dequeue(), Q = {6, 7}. After Enqueue(Y), Q = {6, 7, Y}. After Dequeue(), Q = {7, Y}.

questions.q: Consider a queue Q = {25, 50, 75} with operations as follows: Dequeue(), Enqueue(60), Dequeue(). What will be the new queue?
What is the time complexity of the dequeue operation in a queue?
A queue Q has the following operations: Enqueue(A), Enqueue(B), Dequeue(), Dequeue(). What will be the final queue if initially Q = {10, 20}?
questions.answer: Initially Q = {10, 20}. After Enqueue(A), Q = {10, 20, A}. After Enqueue(B), Q = {10, 20, A, B}. After Dequeue(), Q = {20, A, B}. After Dequeue(), Q = {A, B}.
